results.prompt_text: Do you prefer studying alone or studying with a small group? Give one reason and one example.
results.transcript_text: I prefer studying with a small group because discussion helps me notice ideas that I would miss on my own. For example, when I prepared for a history quiz with two classmates last month, one friend explained the timeline in a much clearer way than the textbook. As a result, I remembered the details better and felt more confident during the test.

strengths: Response length is already close to a full TOEFL practice turn.
The answer uses causal or example markers instead of listing ideas only.

results.sample_revision: I would answer directly, give one reason, add a concrete example, and end with the result. That structure usually sounds more complete and more TOEFL-ready.
notes: Dry run enabled: outputs were produced without forcing API calls.

results.prompt_text: Some people believe students should spend more time on practical job skills, while others think they should focus on broad academic subjects. Which view do you agree with and why?
results.response_text: I believe students should still spend most of their time on broad academic subjects because strong general knowledge makes later job training easier. For example, students who read widely and practice analytical writing can adapt faster when a company introduces new tools or new workflows. Practical skills matter, but they often change quickly. A broad academic foundation helps people learn, question, and communicate well in many different jobs. For that reason, I think broad academic study is the better long-term investment.
